feed.title: Pronounce — recent dictionary additions
feed.subtitle: How engineers actually pronounce project / product / jargon names. Community-maintained.

entry.title: ANOVA — uh noh vuh
entry.summary: ANOVA is pronounced "uh noh vuh" (/əˈnoʊvə/). Analysis of Variance; "uh-NO-vuh".

entry.title: MANOVA — muh noh vuh
entry.summary: MANOVA is pronounced "muh noh vuh" (/məˈnoʊvə/). Multivariate ANOVA; "muh-NO-vuh".

entry.title: t-test — tee test
entry.summary: t-test is pronounced "tee test" (/ˈtiː ˌtɛst/). Student's t-test; "T-test".

entry.title: chi-squared — kye squared
entry.summary: chi-squared is pronounced "kye squared" (/ˌkaɪ ˈskwɛərd/). χ² test — the chi is the Greek letter, pronounced "kye"; NOT "chee" or "chai".

entry.title: nabla — nahb luh
entry.summary: nabla is pronounced "nahb luh" (/ˈnɑːblə/). The ∇ operator (gradient); "NAH-bluh" or "NAB-luh".

entry.title: KKT — kay kay tee
entry.summary: KKT is pronounced "kay kay tee" (/ˌkeɪ keɪ ˈtiː/). Karush-Kuhn-Tucker conditions; letters "K-K-T".

entry.title: MCMC — em see em see
entry.summary: MCMC is pronounced "em see em see" (/ˌɛm siː ɛm ˈsiː/). Markov Chain Monte Carlo; letters "M-C-M-C".

entry.title: HMM — aitch em em
entry.summary: HMM is pronounced "aitch em em" (/ˌeɪtʃ ɛm ˈɛm/). Hidden Markov Model; letters "H-M-M".

entry.title: SVM — ess vee em
entry.summary: SVM is pronounced "ess vee em" (/ˌɛs viː ˈɛm/). Support Vector Machine; letters "S-V-M".

entry.title: KNN — kay en en
entry.summary: KNN is pronounced "kay en en" (/ˌkeɪ ɛn ˈɛn/). k-Nearest Neighbors; letters "K-N-N".

entry.title: PCA — pee see ay
entry.summary: PCA is pronounced "pee see ay" (/ˌpiː siː ˈeɪ/). Principal Component Analysis; letters "P-C-A".

entry.title: ICA — eye see ay
entry.summary: ICA is pronounced "eye see ay" (/ˌaɪ siː ˈeɪ/). Independent Component Analysis; letters "I-C-A".

entry.title: t-SNE — tee ess en ee
entry.summary: t-SNE is pronounced "tee ess en ee" (/ˌtiː ɛs ɛn ˈiː/). t-distributed Stochastic Neighbor Embedding; letters or "tee-snee".

entry.title: UMAP — yoo map
entry.summary: UMAP is pronounced "yoo map" (/ˈjuːmæp/). Uniform Manifold Approximation and Projection; "YOO-map".

entry.title: sutton — suht un
entry.summary: sutton is pronounced "suht un" (/ˈsʌtən/). Richard Sutton (RL, "The Bitter Lesson"); "SUH-tun".

entry.title: silver — sil ver
entry.summary: silver is pronounced "sil ver" (/ˈsɪlvər/). David Silver (DeepMind, AlphaGo); "SIL-ver".

entry.title: barto — bar toh
entry.summary: barto is pronounced "bar toh" (/ˈbɑːrtoʊ/). Andrew Barto (RL textbook co-author with Sutton); "BAR-toh".

entry.title: vapnik — vap nik
entry.summary: vapnik is pronounced "vap nik" (/ˈvæpnɪk/). Vladimir Vapnik (VC dimension, SVM); "VAP-nik".

entry.title: bottou — boo too
entry.summary: bottou is pronounced "boo too" (/buːˈtuː/). Léon Bottou (SGD, large-scale learning); French "boo-TOO".

entry.title: rumelhart — roo mul hart
entry.summary: rumelhart is pronounced "roo mul hart" (/ˈruːməlˌhɑːrt/). David Rumelhart (backprop, PDP); "ROO-mul-hart".

entry.title: hopfield — hop feeld
entry.summary: hopfield is pronounced "hop feeld" (/ˈhɒpfiːld/). John Hopfield (Hopfield networks; 2024 Nobel in Physics); "HOP-feeld".

entry.title: judea-pearl — joo dee uh purl
entry.summary: judea-pearl is pronounced "joo dee uh purl" (/dʒuːˈdiːə ˈpɜːrl/). Judea Pearl (causal inference, Bayesian networks); "joo-DEE-uh PURL".

entry.title: boltzmann — bohlts mun
entry.summary: boltzmann is pronounced "bohlts mun" (/ˈboʊltsmən/). Ludwig Boltzmann (statistical mechanics, Boltzmann machine); "BOHLTS-mun".

entry.title: schölkopf — shurl kopf
entry.summary: schölkopf is pronounced "shurl kopf" (/ˈʃɜːlkɒpf/). Bernhard Schölkopf (kernel methods, causal ML); "SHURL-kopf" (ö ≈ "ur"); English speakers often say "SHELL-kopf".

entry.title: tegmark — teg mark
entry.summary: tegmark is pronounced "teg mark" (/ˈtɛɡmɑːrk/). Max Tegmark (MIT, AI safety, Future of Life Institute); "TEG-mark".
